touches.ai_summary: Sent LinkedIn connection request with personalized message about call recording solutions for sales teams. No response received.
Attempted cold call to Cameron Wilson. No answer, left voicemail introducing our conversation intelligence platform and mentioning specific use case for eCommerce sales teams.
Follow-up email referencing previous attempts. Cameron responded expressing interest in learning more about call recording and coaching capabilities for their sales team.

questions_asked.question: What makes your solution different from Gong?
questions_asked.answer: Explained our unlimited automation tier and coaching-focused analytics that help managers scale 1:1 feedback across distributed teams.

touches.ai_summary: Brief discovery call with Cameron. He's leading sales for BeaconSolutions and looking for ways to improve rep performance. Currently evaluating tools. Expressed interest in call recording and coaching automation.

questions_asked.question: What's driving the search for a conversation intelligence tool right now?
questions_asked.answer: Cameron mentioned they're scaling the sales team and need better visibility into rep performance and deal progression.

questions_asked.question: How quickly can we get this up and running?
questions_asked.answer: Explained typical onboarding is 1-2 weeks with our implementation team.

touches.ai_summary: Sent calendar invite for demo and overview materials. Cameron confirmed timing works and shared he's looking at Gong as well but wants to see our coaching features in action.
Pre-demo email confirming attendees and agenda. Cameron confirmed he'll be the only one joining, mentioned his VP is traveling but trusts his evaluation.

questions_asked.question: Would it be valuable to have your VP or any other stakeholders join the demo?
questions_asked.answer: Cameron said he'll brief leadership after seeing it himself first.

touches.ai_summary: Product demo focused on call recording, AI-generated coaching insights, and performance dashboards. Cameron engaged throughout, particularly interested in automated coaching scorecards. Asked detailed questions about integrations and reporting. Mentioned Gong pricing seems high for their team size.

questions_asked.question: Does this integrate with our existing CRM and dialers?
questions_asked.answer: Confirmed native integrations with major CRMs and dialer platforms.

questions_asked.question: Can managers customize the coaching criteria?
questions_asked.answer: Walked through custom scorecard builder and AI prompt configuration.

questions_asked.question: How many seats are you thinking for the initial rollout?
questions_asked.answer: Cameron mentioned 9 seats to start, covering their core sales team.

next_steps: Cameron to discuss internally this week

touches.ai_summary: Sent proposal for 9 seats on unlimited automation tier at $10,500 ACV. Cameron responded asking about pricing flexibility given their stage and comparing to Gong's offer.

questions_asked.question: Is there room to work on price? Gong came in lower and we're watching budget closely.
questions_asked.answer: Explained we can explore options on a call to find the right fit for their needs.

touches.ai_summary: Proposal review call. Cameron liked the platform but pushed back on pricing, citing Gong's lower quote and their smaller team size. Discussed value of unlimited automation vs competitors' tiered pricing. He's sole decision-maker but wants to move quickly.

questions_asked.question: What would make this a no-brainer for you?
questions_asked.answer: Cameron said getting closer to $6-7K range would seal it, especially since they're early stage.

questions_asked.question: What's the minimum commitment period?
questions_asked.answer: Explained standard annual contract with quarterly payment options.

objections: Pricing is higher than Gong's offer for similar seat count

touches.ai_summary: Negotiation call to discuss pricing adjustments. Offered 25% discount to $7,875 ACV. Cameron appreciated the movement but said Gong is at $6K and he needs to justify the premium. Discussed ROI and coaching automation value. He's motivated to close this month but price-sensitive.

questions_asked.question: Beyond price, is there anything else blocking a decision?
questions_asked.answer: Cameron confirmed the product fits their needs well, just needs pricing to align with budget.

questions_asked.question: Can we do a shorter pilot period instead of annual commitment?
questions_asked.answer: Explained our pricing is built for annual commitments but can explore payment terms.

questions_asked.question: When does your team need this in place?
questions_asked.answer: Cameron mentioned they're hiring 3 new reps next month and want coaching infrastructure ready.

objections: Still higher than competitive offer, hard to justify the delta internally
next_steps: Get manager approval for further discount
Circle back by end of week

touches.ai_summary: Final pricing call after manager approval. Presented $6,100 ACV (37% discount) to match urgency and budget. Cameron accepted, appreciated the flexibility. Discussed contract terms and implementation timeline. He'll handle procurement internally and expects to sign early next week.

questions_asked.question: What does onboarding look like and how much time will my team need to invest?
questions_asked.answer: Outlined 2-week implementation with dedicated CSM, ~4 hours total time investment from their team.

questions_asked.question: What's your internal approval process look like from here?
questions_asked.answer: Cameron confirmed he has budget authority and will route contract through their legal team.

questions_asked.question: Any flexibility on payment terms?
questions_asked.answer: Agreed to quarterly invoicing to help with cash flow.
